search_queries_used: site:gov.ae lower urinary tract symptoms guideline UAE
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/cg97 lower urinary tract symptoms men initial assessment PSA urinalysis
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/cg97 urinary retention post void residual referral
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
population_applicability: Partial: CG97 is for men aged 18 and over, while the legacy workflow has no explicit sex or age filter.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for selected initial, specialist, and retention documentation, not diagnosis, treatment selection, or surgery.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE LUTS guideline was identified. NICE service, PSA, referral, and treatment pathways require UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: The official NICE recommendations page, last reviewed 2024-12-19, was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: CG97 remains current official NICE guidance.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult male LUTS history, abdominal or bladder or rectal examination, urinalysis, qualified PSA or creatinine, conservative-advice, referral, follow-up, and combined-history fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped urinary-flow and post-void-residual documentation in specialist assessment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports specialist history and examination, urinary flow, post-void residual, and recommendation-qualified imaging or cystoscopy context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped acute-retention prompts without mapping catheter, medicine, or surgery instructions.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports acute or chronic urinary-retention context, palpable or percussable bladder, residual volume, creatinine, qualified imaging, and clinician-led follow-up or referral; catheter and medicine instructions are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: The workflow does not encode adult male eligibility, symptom severity, specialist setting, renal-impairment suspicion, cancer-risk, or retention qualifiers.

Fever, neurological examination, new onset after age 50, broad vitals, previous records, generic clinician plan, medication plan, fixed follow-up intervals, PRN wording, and automatic BPH diagnosis remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, PSA interpretation, medicine selection or dose, catheter care, surgery, referral urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
